why_line: Bessent keeps the Economic Fury hammer swinging at Iran's LPG and shadow banking right up to deal day — the sanctions architecture that decides how fast, or slow, Iranian crude actually comes back to market.

Trump swaps the chaos of acting-DNI Pulte for Wall Street's Jay Clayton, and even Warner blesses the temperament — a rare clean confirmation lane that ends the bipartisan fury over the intelligence chair.
Trump's own party hands him a Labor Day clock on Iran — the first time his allies have put a hard deadline on his war, and the rebuke is the story.
Musk closes the largest IPO in history up 19% and crosses the trillion line alone — the first human to do it, and no one else even in the frame.
Washington pulls the foreign plug on Anthropic's two flagship models — the first government recall of a commercial AI, and it lands squarely on the lab that built its brand on being the safe one.
Beijing forces Zuckerberg to tear apart a $2B deal he already closed — the first completed US-China AI acquisition unwound by fiat, and Meta eats the loss.
Ellison's AI capex blew past estimates again and the credit market flinched — a second straight bruise after Oracle's 8.5% plunge earlier this week, the debt bill coming due in public.
Pichai goes on offense, suing a Chinese cybercrime ring for weaponizing Gemini and planting a flag on AI-misuse liability while the rest of SV plays defense.
Sacks publicly torches Anthropic's regulatory pitch from inside the White House — Amodei's safety play loses its Washington patron on the same day the feds pull his models.
Zuckerberg's months-old AI unit is already a 'soul-crushing gulag' on the brink of exodus — paired with the forced Manus unwind, his whole AI bet is bleeding from the inside.
A coordinated state-AG probe lands on OpenAI's health-data handling just as Altman files for IPO — the first multi-state AI enforcement action, and the worst possible timing for the roadshow.

Hegseth puts a Cabinet face on the Anthropic beatdown and Mark Kelly hands him bipartisan cover — Amodei's safety pitch is now a national-security liability, with the White House meeting next week as the only off-ramp.
The people closest to the machine quietly pocketed $14B before the public ever bought a share — a bearish insider tell hanging over Altman's IPO that no growth chart erases.

Trump claims the Iran deal done and dated for Friday signing — after war-powers rebukes and a Labor Day deadline boxed him in, he finally has a headline, even if Graham, Reed, Jeffries and Pence all doubt it holds.
